info.title: Plot status dropdown + PUT /api/plots/{id}/status UI update

parts.text:
Update manage/[id].jsx.

Add dropdown in each plot card to change status.
On change, send PUT request to /api/plots/{plot_id}/status.
Update UI after success.
Keep implementation simple.
The user wants me to update a file called manage/[id].jsx to add a dropdown in each plot card to change the status. Let me first read the file to understand its structure.

state.title: frontend/src/pages/admin/manage/[id].jsx

parts.text:
The implementation looks complete:
1. Added STATUSES constant with possible statuses
2. Added `updating` state to track which plot is being updated
3. Added `handleStatusChange` function that sends PUT request to `/api/plots/${plotId}/status` and updates UI on success
4. Replaced the status text with a dropdown select
5. Added basic styling for the select

The implementation is simple and functional.

Done. Added a dropdown in each plot card with statuses (Available, Booked, Sold, Reserved). On change, it sends a PUT request to `/api/plots/{plot_id}/status` and updates the UI on success. The dropdown is disabled while updating to prevent multiple simultaneous requests.
